content:
Meet Sol, Terra, and Luna: OpenAI’s most capable model family shipped to roughly 20 government-approved partners. The restriction may matter more than the model.

7 min read
Just now

--

OpenAI just shipped a new frontier model family, and for most of us the headline isn’t what it can do. It’s that we’re not allowed to touch it yet.
On Friday, June 26, the company began a limited preview of GPT-5.6 — three models called Sol, Terra, and Luna — and, at the U.S. government’s request, restricted the launch to a small circle of “trusted partners” whose participation was cleared with Washington. That’s a first for a mainstream OpenAI release, and it tells you as much about where AI is heading as any benchmark score.
Here’s what actually launched, why it’s locked down, and what it means for builders, businesses, and anyone who depends on these tools.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

What happened
GPT-5.6 is a three-model family under a new naming system. The number (5.6) marks the generation; the names mark durable capability tiers that can each improve on their own schedule:

• Sol — the flagship, OpenAI’s most capable model yet.
